title: Pyrus floccosa

The classification of Pyrus floccosa is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, the division Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida. intro: Pyrus floccosa, also known as the Mongolian pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of northern China. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus floccosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus floccosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders, as well as to promote overall well-being.
